page.content: In recent years, the analysis of transportation efficiency and its environmental impact has become increasingly important, particularly in urban settings. The data presented in Table 1 provides a comprehensive overview of key performance metrics related to bus operations over several years, specifically focusing on kilometers traveled (km) and fuel consumption measured in liters per kilometer (lit/km). Notably, the table includes data from 1987, 2002, 2005, and 2007, allowing for a longitudinal comparison of operational efficiency and changes in separation conditions.

lines.text: In recent years, the analysis of transportation efficiency and its environmental impact has become increasingly important,
particularly in urban settings. The data presented in Table 1 provides a comprehensive overview of key performance metrics
related to bus operations over several years, specifically focusing on kilometers traveled (km) and fuel consumption measured
in liters per kilometer (lit/km). Notably, the table includes data from 1987, 2002, 2005, and 2007, allowing for a longitudinal
comparison of operational efficiency and changes in separation conditions.

page.html:
GC separation conditions
InjectionMultimode (PTV) injection, splitless mode
Injection volume2 μl
Carrier gasHelium; 1 ml/min (50 min), then 2 ml/min (until the end of the analytical run)
ColumnAgilent DB-EUPAH (60 m × 250 mm × 0.25 μm)
Oven temperature programme50 °C (0.8 min), ramp 45 °C/min to 200 °C, ramp 2.5 °C/min to 225 °C, ramp 5 °C/min to 266 °C, ramp 14 °C/min to 300 °C, ramp 10 °C/min up to 320 °C (48 min). Total run time 74.762 min

cells.cell_text: Helium; 1 ml/min (50 min), then 2 ml/min (until the end of the analytical run)
50 °C (0.8 min), ramp 45 °C/min to 200 °C, ramp 2.5 °C/min to 225 °C, ramp 5 °C/min to 266 °C, ramp 14 °C/min to 300 °C, ramp 10 °C/min up to 320 °C (48 min). Total run time 74.762 min

page.content: The findings indicate a significant evolution in bus performance, with the operational metrics revealing trends that may correlate with advancements in technology and changes in regulatory standards. Furthermore, the table highlights the importance of monitoring calcium levels expressed in mg/kg/day, which is crucial for assessing the environmental impact of transportation systems. The optimal range for calcium intake, as indicated, suggests a focus on maintaining health and safety standards within the operational framework of public transportation. This data serves as a vital resource for policymakers and transportation planners aiming to enhance the sustainability of bus services in urban environments.
